title: C Multi-Dimensional Arrays
content:
### Multi-Dimensional Arrays in C

C supports multi-dimensional arrays, most commonly 2D arrays (matrices).

### Declaration

```c
type arrayName[rows][columns];
```

### Initialization

```c
int matrix[2][3] = {
  {1, 2, 3},
  {4, 5, 6}
};
// Or flat initialization:
int matrix[2][3] = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6};
```

### Accessing Elements

```c
matrix[row][col]
```

### Memory Layout

Multi-dimensional arrays are stored in row-major order (all of row 0, then all of row 1, etc.).

| Row | Column | Index | Value |
|-----|--------|-------|-------|
| 0 | 0 | `[0][0]` | 1 |
| 0 | 1 | `[0][1]` | 2 |
| 0 | 2 | `[0][2]` | 3 |
| 1 | 0 | `[1][0]` | 4 |
| 1 | 1 | `[1][1]` | 5 |
| 1 | 2 | `[1][2]` | 6 |

---

> **Key Point:** When passing 2D arrays to functions, you must specify the column size in the parameter: `void func(int arr[][3])`.

quiz.question: How are 2D arrays stored in memory?
